verdicts.missing: Unexpectedness is replaced by mere prior ignorance that an event is happening.
verdicts.reason: It describes a visibility change, but not knowing of an occurrence before it occurs does not make that occurrence unexpected.

verdicts.missing: A thing's unexpected becoming-visible is replaced by a generic event that people come to see.
verdicts.reason: It miscasts the apparition itself as the visual stimulus and omits the unexpectedness criterion.

verdicts.missing: Unexpectedness is replaced by people not thinking about the thing.
verdicts.reason: The before-and-after visibility skeleton is apt, but not thinking of something does not mean not expecting its appearance.

verdicts.missing: The unexpected character of the becoming-visible is absent.
verdicts.reason: It gives only a generic transition from not visible to visible, which also fits expected appearances.
It renders an event in which someone unexpectedly comes to be able to see a thing that they could not see before.
